description: A series of massive, peaceful protests in South Korea starting in October 2016 calling for the impeachment of President Park Geun-hye following the Choi Soon-sil scandal. The protests culminated in her constitutional court-ordered removal from office in March 2017, marking a pivotal moment for civil rights and democratic accountability in South Korea.

key_events.description: October 24, 2016: JTBC news reports tablet discovery linking Choi Soon-sil to secret government influence.
November 12, 2016: Over one million protesters gather in Gwanghwamun Square, Seoul.
December 9, 2016: National Assembly votes to impeach Park Geun-hye.
March 10, 2017: Constitutional Court upholds impeachment, removing Park from office.

calibration_notes: This scenario demonstrates a rapid polarization shift from government support to widespread civil unrest and eventual institutional success, providing a high-variance example of opinion volatility.
